title: SNDL Shares Rise as It Achieves Positive Free Cash Flow, Announces Share Buybacks
text: Sundial Growers Inc. (NASDAQ: SNDL) announced positive free cash flow for its fiscal third quarter and approved a share buyback program renewal, causing shares to climb 1.4% to $1.45 in early Monday trading. The company's net revenue grew 3.1% to C$237.6 million for the three months ending September 30, 2023. Net losses contracted substantially to C$21.8 million from C$98.8 million year-over-year, primarily due to asset impairments recorded in 2022. Most significantly, SNDL generated C$16.5 million in free cash flow, a dramatic turnaround from negative C$67.1 million in the prior-year quarter. This performance was supported by the company's 2022 acquisition of Alcanna, which transformed SNDL into Canada's largest private-sector liquor and cannabis retailer. The board authorized repurchasing up to C$100 million of company stock. Chief Executive Officer Zach George stated the milestone reflected commitment to operational and financial excellence, adding the firm remains focused on improved profitability, material growth and greater efficiencies.
